name: Dijkstra's Shortest Path
def: Computes single-source shortest paths on graphs with non-negative edge weights.
problem: Find minimum-cost routes from one node to all others when edges have non-negative costs.
mechanism: Greedily expands the closest unsettled node using a priority queue, relaxing outgoing edges; once settled, a node’s distance is final.
benefits: O((V+E) log V) with a binary heap
limits: Explores in all directions (A* with a heuristic is faster for point-to-point)
failures: Silent wrong answers on negative weights
Priority-queue staleness if not handled (lazy deletion)
whenNot: Single target with a good heuristic available (prefer A*)
myths: "Works on any weighted graph" — only non-negative weights
